title: Hangul syllabic-block model
context: Hangul is the script of the Korean language and is composed of syllabic blocks (onset consonant + vowel + optional coda), each fitting within a square cell. Correctly modeling Hangul requires understanding block composition, stroke order within each jamo (individual character component), and grid-based guideline geometry. Wrong stroke order in Hangul practice sheets would reinforce incorrect motor habits. This task is blocked on securing a script-literate Korean reviewer.
objective: Model the Hangul jamo (initial consonants, vowels, final consonants) and representative syllabic block compositions as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef and grid-based guideline system, using a gate-verified open Hangul font and with script-literate reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: BLOCKER: A script-literate Hangul reviewer (native/fluent Korean writer or qualified teacher) is named and confirmed.
Hangul jamo modeled: initial consonants (초성), vowels (중성), and final consonants (종성), plus representative syllabic block compositions — all as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef.
Grid-based guideline system (square cell per syllabic block) defined in GuidelineSystem and implemented in the renderer.
Gate-verified open Hangul font sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact attached); SOURCES.md committed with provenance and snapshots.
Script-literate reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist confirming stroke order within each jamo and block composition matches the authoritative source.
Sheets render in A4 and US Letter; CC-BY-4.0 with attribution; provenance-gate green.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M4, section 2 (Hangul syllabic-block model), section 5 (grid guides for CJK)
output: data/hangul/ directory with schema-valid jamo and block GlyphModel JSON files, SOURCES.md, grid-based guideline system, reviewer-signed checklist, and trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0.
